title: ftCo_Attack100 matches

body: Using a different model this time to see if I can be more token efficient. I noticed that this output a lot more `temp` variable names, which I'm not a fan of

title: ftCo_Attack100 matches
author: PsiLupan
body: Let's just not do inline assembly. It was common practice when we needed entire files pre-objdiff, but really isn't necessary now outside of SDK.
diff_hunk:
@@ -2303,34 +2303,234 @@ void ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Anim(Fighter_GObj* gobj)
 #pragma dont_inline on
 void ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_IASA(Fighter_GObj* gobj) {}
 
-static inline void ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline(Fighter_GObj* gobj)
+#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO
+
+asm void ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys(Fighter_GObj* gobj)

title: ftCo_Attack100 matches
author: PsiLupan
body: Think if the last `#pragma pop` issue is addressed, it should be good to go.
